14.reason: [The program incorrectly assumes polynomial ends with a zero exponent. Input termination is by newline, not by exponent 0.]

15.reason: The loop condition assumes input ends with a zero exponent term, but the input ends with newline. For the given test case, after reading the first term, n is 1000000000 (non-zero), so the loop continues incorrectly, reading more input than intended and eventually causing an infinite loop or wrong parsing.

29.reason: This line attempts to read the next coefficient even when the current exponent is non-zero, but it does not check for end-of-line. It will consume the next coefficient of the next polynomial or cause undefined behavior when no more input is available, leading to an infinite loop or incorrect data.
